expectations.text: The artifact content follows a reusable template shape and includes implementation slices, file-level map, and validation plan.

expectations.text: Artifact metadata fields are concretely filled (no placeholder bracket values) for status, track, and next step.

expectations.text: Each referenced file in Relevant Findings includes why that file matters.

expectations.text: Open questions are tightly bounded to truly blocking items and include risk framing.

expectations.text: Provides artifact status in the response with the exact artifact path.

expectations.text: Includes rationale or trade-offs for the proposed design.

notes: Whether to enforce same-turn Standard-track artifact creation remains unresolved.

All 8 expectations pass in both configurations, so this benchmark still does not differentiate skill value.
Metadata originally declared runs_per_configuration as 3, but only one run per configuration was recorded; summaries are effectively n=1.
time_seconds is 0.0 for all runs, so timing data is not informative.
with_skill used more tool calls (23 vs 20) but fewer tokens (13154 vs 13992), indicating mixed resource trade-offs in this single sample.
The old_skill run recorded 1 error yet still passed all assertions, suggesting current checks do not fully cover that failure mode.
Only eval_id 2 is present, so there is no cross-eval evidence of generalization.
